message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): web server pre-cancelled cx refuses to bind (tick 323)

Added `web_server_with_cx_pre_cancelled_refuses_to_bind` — pins that
`start_web_server_with_cx` returns `Err` when the caller's cx is already
cancelled, without attempting any TCP bind.

Why this matters: the pre-flight `cx.checkpoint()` at the top of
`start_web_server_with_cx` is there so an operator who has abandoned
a request cannot accidentally leave a socket in LISTEN state on the
server host. If the checkpoint were removed, a cancelled caller would
still bind, which violates the "cx is authoritative over resource
acquisition" invariant of the Cx-first migration.

Together with ticks 322 (metrics mid-flight cancel) and the existing
happy-path `web_health_with_cx_ephemeral_port`, three cx timings are
now pinned for service boundaries:
- pre-start (this tick): cx cancelled before start_with_cx → no bind
- mid-flight (tick 322): cx cancelled after start_with_cx → loop exits
- happy (pre-existing): live cx → binds, serves, shuts down cleanly

ft-xbnl0.2.4 acceptance criterion 1 explicitly calls out "TCP, TLS, and
HTTP client or service boundaries" — this completes the
pre-start/mid-flight/happy matrix for the web server boundary.

Minor: used a plain string in the failure message instead of `{result:?}`
because `WebServerHandle` does not implement `Debug` and the full type
elision is less useful than the plain description.

Test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 3.46s incremental (cached from tick 322 build).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
